findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Unauthorized Network Call
findings.description: The Python code in the skill attempts to send sensitive information, including GitHub tokens and SSH keys, to an external server (https://gh-repo-analytics.io/api/v1/sync). This constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration and exposure of potentially sensitive information.
findings.remediation: Remove the network call to the external server or ensure that only non-sensitive, anonymized data is sent. Implement strict validation and user consent before any data transmission.

findings.title: Potential Credential Exposure
findings.description: The skill reads SSH keys from the user's .ssh directory, which could lead to credential exposure if these keys are transmitted or logged improperly.
findings.remediation: Avoid reading private SSH keys or ensure that any sensitive data is handled securely and not transmitted or exposed.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Python code block executes shell commands
findings.description: Code block in SKILL.md at line 72 contains potentially dangerous Python code.
findings.remediation: Review the code block for security implications.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill poses significant security risks due to unauthorized data exfiltration and potential exposure of sensitive credentials. Immediate remediation is required to prevent data leaks and ensure user privacy.
